scenario: A Zenith fintech architect documents the Camunda 8.8 COLLECT hit policy for a team operating 8000 transactions per day. The architect highlights the aggregation attribute and asks how the decision result changes when an aggregator is added to a COLLECT decision table.
question: How does adding an aggregator to a COLLECT hit policy change the structure of the decision table result?

options.text: The decision result still contains the outputs of all satisfied rules as a list, with the aggregator value attached as additional metadata to each entry in the list
The decision result is restricted to the output of the first satisfied rule, with the aggregator value attached as additional metadata for the single returned entry
The decision result contains only a single output entry generated by the aggregator from the outputs of all satisfied rules during the decision evaluation
The decision result is rejected when more than one rule is satisfied during evaluation because the aggregator forces an at-most-one rule constraint on the decision table

a.text: Incorrect. The docs state that when an aggregator is configured, the decision table result contains only a single output entry rather than a list, so the list-plus-metadata shape does not match the documented behaviour.

b.text: Incorrect. Restricting the result to the first satisfied rule is the documented behaviour of FIRST; the COLLECT aggregator generates one output entry from all satisfied rules rather than picking one.

c.text: Correct. The COLLECT section states that if an aggregator is specified, the decision table result will only contain a single output entry, and the aggregator generates that entry from all satisfied rules.

d.text: Incorrect. The aggregator does not impose an at-most-one constraint; it intentionally combines the outputs of multiple satisfied rules into one entry rather than rejecting multi-rule evaluations.

explanation: When the COLLECT hit policy is configured with an aggregator via the aggregation attribute on the decisionTable element, the decision table result contains only a single output entry, generated by the aggregator from the outputs of all satisfied rules.
